content_preview.title: Veeam Previews Data Platform v13.1 at VeeamON 2026, Launching DataAI Command Platform and Post-Quantum Cryptography Support
content_preview.summary: Veeam used its VeeamON 2026 conference in New York City to preview v13.1 of its Data Platform with 70+ new features, a new DataAI Command Platform, and post-quantum cryptography support.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Veeam Software used its annual VeeamON user conference in New York City on May 12, 2026, to preview Veeam Data Platform v13.1 — a release carrying more than 70 new features — alongside a new DataAI Command Platform that the company described as the infrastructure layer enterprises need to deploy AI trustworthily. The products are scheduled to reach general availability in early Q3 2026, [according to the company's press release](https://www.veeam.com/company/press-release/veeam-prev...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, thorough News article with appropriate technical depth. The article correctly covers VeeamON 2026 as a preview (not a launch) and maintains a clear distinction between announced and generally-available features. The 'What We Don't Know' section is a strength, transparently noting gaps in pricing and GA timing. Word count (983) is within the News category range (400-1200).
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources verified via local snapshots. source-0.html.gz (Veeam press release): confirmed headline claims, Rehan Jalil quote, GA timeline, 550,000 customers, 82% Fortune 500. source-1.html.gz (Veeam blog - v13.1 announcements): confirmed hypervisor expansion (Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization, Sangfor aSV, Vates XCP-ng, Citrix XenServer), 95% hypervisor coverage, PQC support, AD Forest Recovery, Backup Admin Agent details. source-2.html.gz (SiliconANGLE): confirmed three-era framing ('assume restore', 'assume breach', 'assume autonomy'), 82:1 agent ratio, 250,000 non-human identities, 97% excessive privileges, McKinsey partnership for maturity model, $2B ARR, 550,000 customers in 150+ countries, 82 of the Fortune 500. source-3.html.gz (StorageReview): confirmed DataAI Command Platform details (five domains), DataAI Command Graph, DataAI Resilience Module capabilities, 300 senior leaders for maturity model research, four pillars (visibility, enforcement, resilience, data readiness), 12 dimensions, 49 sub-dimensions. source-4.html.gz (Veeam blog - what's new): confirmed 57% ransomware data recovery figure linked to Data Trust and Resilience Report 2026. source-5.html.gz (The NAS Guy): confirmed PQC details, AD Forest Recovery details, Backup Admin Agent capabilities - note this source appears to mirror source-1 content closely. source-6.html.gz (Compare the Cloud): confirmed Eswaran quote verbatim, 300 connectors, 100+ regulatory frameworks. One minor citation issue found: the article attributes the McKinsey partnership to StorageReview and Compare the Cloud, but neither source mentions McKinsey - it is documented only in SiliconANGLE (source-2), which is already cited in the article.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified: 70+ new features, Q3 2026 GA, DataAI Command Platform built on Securiti AI acquisition, 300+ connectors, 100+ regulatory frameworks, 82:1 AI agent ratio, 250,000 non-human identities, 97% excessive privileges, 57% ransomware recovery rate, 82% Fortune 500, 550,000 customers, $2B ARR, 12 dimensions, 49 sub-dimensions across five maturity levels. One minor misattribution: McKinsey partnership for the Data and AI Trust Maturity Model is correctly a real fact (confirmed in SiliconANGLE) but the article cites StorageReview and Compare the Cloud for this claim - neither of those sources names McKinsey. A corrections note is warranted.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. Appropriately attributes Veeam's marketing claims as company statements rather than editorial assertions. The Analysis section is appropriately skeptical ('Whether a backup-heritage company can successfully occupy the AI trust infrastructure role').
editor_notes.originality: No prior Veeam coverage found in the archive. This is a genuinely new topic - VeeamON 2026 announcements have not been covered previously.
concerns: The McKinsey attribution for the Data and AI Trust Maturity Model is credited to StorageReview and Compare the Cloud, but neither source names McKinsey. The McKinsey partnership is confirmed only in SiliconANGLE (source-2). This is a citation misattribution for a true fact and should be filed as a corrections record.
recommendations: Add veeam.com, storagereview.com, and comparethecloud.net to the source allowlist if they are to be used regularly as enterprise technology sources.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid, well-researched News article covering VeeamON 2026 announcements. All major claims are source-verified. One minor citation misattribution (McKinsey in maturity model credited to wrong sources) justifies APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS rather than APPROVE.
